calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-24-2026 Set For Trial On Feb-22-27 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jun-29-2026 Text Ruling
TENTATIVE RULING:

Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, June 29, 2026, Line 5.

2 - Plaintiff Dana Valois's unopposed Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART.

(The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties).

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order quoting the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing.

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.
=(302/JMQ)


MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Aaron Bartz, Esq. 949-504-4413 Bartz Law Group for Plaintiff.
Allison Fernandez, Esq. 415-536-3404 Ogletree Deakins for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.

Mr. Bartz agrees with the TR.
The Court comments and poses questions; there is no opposition before me, check the docket, operate on discovery act, communicate more.
Ms. Fernandez contests and presents arguments; misunderstanding motion withdrawn, served supplemental responses last May, ongoing process
Mr. Bartz responds; nothing further to add, requests all reponses, privilege log.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

2 - Plaintiff Dana Valois's unopposed Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART.

(see word minutes for full details)

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order quoting the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

TENTATIVE RULING:

(The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties).

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order quoting the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing.

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.
=(302/JMQ)


MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Aaron Bartz, Esq. 949-504-4413 Bartz Law Group for Plaintiff.
Allison Fernandez, Esq. 415-536-3404 Ogletree Deakins for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.

Mr. Bartz agrees with the TR.
The Court comments and poses questions; there is no opposition before me, check the docket, operate on discovery act, communicate more.
Ms. Fernandez contests and presents arguments; misunderstanding motion withdrawn, served supplemental responses last May, ongoing process
Mr. Bartz responds; nothing further to add, requests all reponses, privilege log.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

1 - Plaintiff Dana Valois's unopposed Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART.

(see word minutes for full details)

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order quoting the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
